evidence.quote: The deadly cross‑talk between Hippo pathway and epithelial–mesenchymal transition (EMT) in cancer
This review is based on PubMed searches... 85 publications were selected for full-text review.
describe the existing evidence of cross talk between Hippo signaling pathway key molecules and the process of EMT, with emphasis on the role of Hippo—EMT interplay in cancer.

evidence.quote: YAP was correlated with EMT in colorectal [9, 10], breast [11]... expression of mesenchymal markers such as vimentin and N-cadherin correlated negatively with the expression of epithelial markers such as E-cadherin
Snail and Slug... form complexes with the Hippo pathway transcriptional co-activators YAP and TAZ... ZEB1 was also shown to directly interact with Hippo transcriptional co-activator YAP.
E-cadherin is the major component in adhjerens junctions... Claudin6 was shown to interact with LATS1/2... CAPG... has been shown to promote nuclear aggregation of YAP
Scribble is a major gatekeeper of epithelial polarity... translocation of Scribble from the membrane into the cytoplasm... SnoN binds to Hippo pathway molecules... and inhibits TAZ phosphorylation
EMT-related molecules are included in the list of YAP/TAZ target genes... epigenetic modifications, including microRNAs... EZH2 is a key molecule in silencing the expression of WWC1
Hippo signaling pathway components represent promising therapeutic targets... YAP1 inhibitor CA3... TAZ inhibitor AR-42... Luteolin

85 publications were selected... YAP was correlated with EMT in colorectal, breast, lung, pancreratic, head and neck, esophageal, endometrial, ovarian, gastric, brain cancer, as well as in hepatocellular carcinoma
In this review, we describe the existing evidence of cross talk between Hippo signaling pathway key molecules and the process of EMT, with emphasis on the role of Hippo—EMT interplay in cancer.
